title: centaurea × levantina
intro: Centaurea × levantina, also known as Levant knapweed, is a hybrid perennial plant in the sunflower family, derived from the cross between Centaurea cineraria and Centaurea iberica. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Centaurea × levantina include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as asthma. The herbal preparations of Centaurea × levantina include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
